title: rootless integration tests run with vfs not $STORAGE_DRIVER
body:
I have noticed that in cirrus-ci a task named "Integration rootless fedora-37 w/ overlay" contains the following output:

> [+3284s] ok 631 overlay specific level # skip skipping overlay test because $STORAGE_DRIVER = vfs
> [+3285s] ok 632 overlay source permissions and owners # skip skipping overlay test because $STORAGE_DRIVER = vfs
> [+3285s] ok 633 overlay path contains colon # skip skipping overlay test because $STORAGE_DRIVER = vfs

Which does not looks correct to me

comments.body:
OK, because the test is re-executed via `ssh $ROOTLESS_USER@localhost`, and ssh does not pass all the environment by default, a bunch of configuration is lost during that step.

Looks like this was added in #3804
Thanks @kolyshkin for noticing. I know exactly what's causing this and have a somewhat-related TODO kicking around to take care of it.

> I know exactly what's causing this

Do you mean something else than ssh? I'm curious.

Nope, we still need to use ssh.  The issue has to do with very old and ugly env. var. processing code.  I'm planning to replace it with something similar to [what we do for podman CI](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/aee7a3c16aee82d11689db91e42f82e52a690d98/contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh#L48-L58) (it fixed the same problem over there).
Oh gosh, forgot about this.  Moving back on my TODO list.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Thanks, I've still got https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4872 on my list.  Though it's near the bottom of my stack.  Is there reason I should bump it more toward the top?
